user_story: As a service owner, I want to list and filter business services so that I can find the services I am responsible for.
expected_behavior: Queries dim_business_services with optional case-insensitive search (name/description ILIKE) plus equality filters for service_classification, tbm_tower, business_criticality, operational_status, and owned_by. Results are ordered by created_at DESC and paginated via page/limit (default 1/50, OFFSET computed). Returns 200 {success:true, data:rows, pagination:{page,limit,total,totalPages}} using a parallel COUNT query; returns 500 {success:false} on DB error.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to retrieve a single business service with its mapping and dependency counts so that I can see its current state at a glance.
expected_behavior: Selects the row from dim_business_services by service_id; returns 404 {success:false,'Business service not found'} when absent. On success it augments the row with mapped_cis_count (COUNT from ci_business_service_mappings) and dependencies_count (COUNT from business_service_dependencies) and returns 200 {success:true,data}. Returns 500 on DB error.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to create a business service record so that I can track a service in the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Inserts into dim_business_services from a 17-column body (service_id, name, description, service_classification, tbm_tower, business_criticality, operational_status defaulting to 'active', service_type, owned_by, managed_by, support_group, service_level_requirement, category, tags, related_ci_types, cost_allocation, metadata); cost_allocation and metadata are JSON.stringify'd when present. Returns 201 {success:true,data:row} with RETURNING *. On Postgres unique violation (code 23505) returns 409 'Business service with this ID already exists'; other errors return 500.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to partially update a business service so that I can keep its attributes current without resending the whole record.
expected_behavior: Builds a dynamic SET clause from the body keys (parameterized values, $2..); returns 400 'No fields to update' when the body is empty. cost_allocation and metadata object values are JSON.stringify'd. Sets updated_at = NOW() and returns the updated row (200 {success:true,data}); returns 404 when no row matches service_id and 500 on DB error.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to delete a business service so that I can remove records that are no longer relevant.
expected_behavior: Deletes from dim_business_services by service_id with RETURNING service_id,name. Returns 404 'Business service not found' when nothing was deleted, otherwise 200 {success:true, message:'Business service deleted successfully', data}. Returns 500 on DB error. Deletion is a hard delete (no soft-delete flag).

feature: List CIs mapped to a service
user_story: As a service owner, I want to see which CIs are mapped to my business service so that I understand what underpins it.
expected_behavior: Selects ci_id, mapping_type, confidence_score and created_at from ci_business_service_mappings filtered by service_id, ordered by created_at DESC. Returns 200 {success:true,data:rows} (empty array when no mappings); returns 500 on DB error. Returns only mapping metadata, not full CI detail.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to bulk-map CIs to a business service so that the service's supporting infrastructure is recorded.
expected_behavior: Reads ci_ids[] plus optional mapping_type (default 'supports') and confidence_score (default 1.0), then bulk-inserts one row per CI into ci_business_service_mappings using ON CONFLICT (ci_id, service_id, mapping_type) DO UPDATE to refresh confidence_score and updated_at. Returns 201 {success:true, message:'N CIs mapped successfully', data:rows}; returns 500 on DB error.

feature: Unmap a CI from a service
user_story: As a service owner, I want to remove a CI mapping so that the service reflects only its real components.
expected_behavior: Deletes the row from ci_business_service_mappings matching service_id and ci_id (RETURNING *). Returns 404 'CI mapping not found' when nothing matched, otherwise 200 {success:true, message:'CI unmapped successfully'}. Returns 500 on DB error.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to see which other business services this service depends on so that I can assess blast radius.
expected_behavior: Joins business_service_dependencies to dim_business_services on depends_on_service_id, returning depends_on_service_id, depends_on_name, service_classification, business_criticality, dependency_type and created_at for the given service_id, ordered by created_at DESC. Returns 200 {success:true,data:rows}; returns 500 on DB error.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to declare that one service depends on another so that dependency mapping stays accurate.
expected_behavior: Inserts into business_service_dependencies (service_id, depends_on_service_id, dependency_type default 'technical') and returns 201 {success:true,data:row}. Returns 409 'This dependency already exists' on unique violation (23505) and 400 'Cannot create circular dependency (service cannot depend on itself)' on check-constraint violation (23514); other errors return 500.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to remove a dependency link so that obsolete relationships are cleaned up.
expected_behavior: Deletes from business_service_dependencies matching service_id and depends_on_service_id (RETURNING *). Returns 404 'Dependency not found' when nothing matched, otherwise 200 {success:true, message:'Dependency deleted successfully'}. Returns 500 on DB error.

user_story: As a service owner, I want incident and change health metrics for a service so that I can judge its operational stability.
expected_behavior: Runs two aggregate queries: incident metrics from fact_business_service_incidents (incidents_7d, incidents_30d, avg_mttr_30d, sla_breaches_30d using FILTER on incident_date windows) and change metrics from fact_business_service_changes (changes_7d, changes_30d, success_rate_30d = successful_count/change_count*100). Returns 200 {success:true,data:{incidents,changes}}; returns 500 on DB error. Aggregate fields return null when no underlying rows exist.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a rolled-up cost summary for a business service so that I can see what the service costs and how that splits by tower.
expected_behavior: Left-joins ci_business_service_mappings to tbm_cost_pools on ci_id for the service, returning ci_count (DISTINCT CIs), total_monthly_cost (SUM of monthly_cost) and cost_by_tower (json_object_agg of resource_tower to summed cost). Returns 200 {success:true,data}; when the service has no mappings it returns the default {ci_count:0, total_monthly_cost:0, cost_by_tower:{}}. Returns 500 on DB error.

feature: Analyze architecture for a CI set
user_story: As an architect, I want to analyze a chosen set of CIs so that I get optimization recommendations for that scope.
expected_behavior: Validates that body.ci_ids is a non-empty array, otherwise returns 400 'ci_ids array is required'. Obtains the architecture optimization engine via getArchitectureOptimizationEngine() and calls engine.analyzeArchitecture(ci_ids), returning 200 {success:true, analysis}. Returns 500 {success:false, error} on engine failure.

user_story: As an architect, I want a cross-service summary of architecture recommendations so that I can prioritize optimization work org-wide.
expected_behavior: Intended to aggregate recommendations across all business services, but the handler is unimplemented: it returns 200 {success:true, message:'Aggregated recommendations endpoint - implementation pending', note:'Use /business-services/:serviceId/analysis...'} with no real data. No engine call is made.
issue_note: Endpoint is a hardcoded placeholder returning 'implementation pending' instead of aggregated recommendations (architecture.routes.ts:85-104).

feature: Analyze architecture for a business service
user_story: As an architect, I want an architecture analysis for a specific business service so that I see recommendations tied to that service's CIs.
expected_behavior: Reads serviceId from the path, gets the optimization engine and calls engine.analyzeBusinessService(serviceId), returning 200 {success:true, analysis}. Returns 500 {success:false, error} on engine failure. No 404 is emitted for unknown services; behavior for a missing service depends on the engine implementation.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a total cost summary across all CIs so that I can report overall IT spend and its tower breakdown.
expected_behavior: Runs two Neo4j Cypher queries over (:CI) nodes: a total (sum of tbm_monthly_cost, count of CIs) and a per-tower breakdown (tbm_resource_tower grouped, summed cost and ciCount, ordered by cost DESC). Returns CostSummary {totalMonthlyCost, totalCIs, costByTower[], currency:'USD', timestamp ISO string}. Throws a GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure; the Neo4j session is always closed.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want costs broken down by resource tower so that I can see where infrastructure spend concentrates.
expected_behavior: Matches (:CI) with non-null tbm_resource_tower and tbm_monthly_cost, optionally filtered to a single tower arg, and returns rows of {tower, subTower, costPool, totalCost (sum), ciCount, topCIs (collected {id,name,cost} capped at 10)} ordered by totalCost DESC. Returns [TowerCost!]!; throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure with the session closed in finally.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want the cost rolled up to a business capability so that I can understand the cost of a capability the business cares about.
expected_behavior: Traverses (BusinessCapability)-[:REALIZES]->(BusinessService)-[:SUPPORTED_BY]->(ApplicationService)-[:DEPENDS_ON|RUNS_ON*1..2]->(CI) to aggregate totalMonthlyCost, ciCount and supportingServices, then runs a second query for the per-tower costByTower breakdown. Throws GraphQLError NOT_FOUND when the capability id does not exist, returns CapabilityCost otherwise, and INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on other failures; session closed in finally.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want costs for a specific business service plus unit economics so that I can see total cost and cost per user.
expected_behavior: Traverses (BusinessService)-[:SUPPORTED_BY]->(ApplicationService)-[:DEPENDS_ON|RUNS_ON*1..2]->(CI) summing tbm_monthly_cost, counting distinct CIs and collecting distinct towers, and reads service.user_count to compute costPerUser (totalCost/userCount, or null when userCount is 0). costPerTransaction is always null (transaction count not available). Throws GraphQLError NOT_FOUND for an unknown service id, INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on other failures; session closed in finally.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want monthly cost trends so that I can see how IT spend changes over time.
expected_behavior: Queries the PostgreSQL ci_snapshot table, grouping by date_trunc('month', snapshot_date) over the last N months (default 6), summing tbm_monthly_cost and counting CIs, ordered by month DESC. Returns [MonthlyCostData!]! of {month, totalCost (parseFloat), ciCount (parseInt)}; throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure. Note: the months value is interpolated directly into the INTERVAL string rather than parameterized (input is numeric per schema).

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want to see how a CI's cost has been allocated so that I can audit chargeback distribution.
expected_behavior: Intended to return a CI's allocation history, but the resolver is a stub: it returns CIAllocationHistory {ciId, totalAllocatedCost:0, allocations:[]} unconditionally with no database query. It therefore always reports zero allocations regardless of any prior allocateCosts call.
issue_note: Resolver is a placeholder (tbm.resolvers.ts:271-279) that returns empty/zero allocations; no allocation persistence/query is implemented.

user_story: As a software asset manager, I want to list software licenses with optional vendor/status filters so that I can review licensing.
expected_behavior: Matches (:CI {type:'software'}) in Neo4j with optional ci.vendor and ci.license_status filters, ordered by ci.license_expiry_date ASC and capped at LIMIT 100, returning each CI's raw properties as SoftwareLicense entries. Throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure; session closed in finally.

user_story: As a software asset manager, I want to see licenses expiring within N days so that I can plan renewals before they lapse.
expected_behavior: Matches (:CI {type:'software'}) where license_expiry_date is between today and today + duration({days}) (default 90), ordered ascending by expiry, returning the CI properties as SoftwareLicense entries. Throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure; session closed in finally.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want to allocate a source CI's cost across target services/capabilities so that shared costs are distributed by a chosen method.
expected_behavior: Validates input (sourceId, targetType, targetIds[] required) throwing BAD_USER_INPUT otherwise, reads the source CI's tbm_monthly_cost from Neo4j (NOT_FOUND if the CI is missing), then computes allocations by method: EQUAL (even split), USAGE_BASED (weighted by allocationRules, falling back to equal), or DIRECT (full cost to first target); default is USAGE_BASED. Returns CostAllocationResult {sourceId, totalCost, allocationMethod, allocations[]}. The computed allocations are NOT persisted, so costAllocations cannot read them back afterward.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want to import general-ledger cost data so that actual financials can be matched to CIs.
expected_behavior: Intended to ingest a GL file (Upload) and match records to CIs, but the resolver is a stub: it ignores the file argument and returns GLImportResult {success:false, recordsImported:0, errorMessage:'GL import feature coming soon'}. No parsing or persistence occurs.
issue_note: Resolver is unimplemented (tbm.resolvers.ts:402-409); always returns success:false 'coming soon' and ignores the Upload arg.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want TBM cost attributes nested on a CI so that I can read a CI's tower, cost pool, monthly cost and depreciation in one query.
expected_behavior: Resolves the tbmAttributes field on CI by reading the parent CI's tbm_* properties; returns null when tbm_resource_tower is absent. Otherwise returns {resourceTower, subTower, costPool, monthlyCost (default 0), costAllocationMethod (default 'USAGE_BASED'), depreciationSchedule (JSON.parse of tbm_depreciation_schedule or null)}.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST cost summary endpoint so that non-GraphQL clients can fetch total IT spend and tower breakdown.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getCostSummary aggregates total tbm_monthly_cost and per-tower costs from Neo4j (mirroring the costSummary GraphQL query) and returns 200 {success:true,data:{totalMonthlyCost,totalCIs,costByTower,currency,timestamp}}. However the route is unreachable: tbm.routes.ts is never imported or mounted in server.ts, so calling it yields a 404 from the app.
issue_note: tbm.routes.ts (tbmRoutes) is never imported/mounted in rest/server.ts (no import line; setupRoutes lines 77-103); entire TBM REST router is dead/unreachable.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint for tower cost breakdown so that I can pull tower-level spend over HTTP.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getCostsByTower returns per-tower aggregates (tower, subTower, costPool, totalCost, ciCount, topCIs) from Neo4j with an optional ?tower filter (validated by towerQuerySchema) as 200 {success:true,data,count}. Unreachable in practice because tbm.routes.ts is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route defined on the unmounted tbmRoutes router (tbm.routes.ts not imported in server.ts); endpoint cannot be reached.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint for capability cost rollups so that HTTP clients can read capability spend.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getCostsByCapability traverses capability->service->app->CI in Neo4j to return totalMonthlyCost, ciCount and supportingServices, with a 404 when the capability id is unknown. Unreachable because the tbmRoutes router is never mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); 404 at the app level for any call.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint for per-service cost so that HTTP clients can read service spend.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getCostsByBusinessService traverses service->app->CI in Neo4j to return totalMonthlyCost, ciCount and towers, with a 404 when the service id is unknown. Unreachable because tbm.routes.ts is not registered in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); endpoint is dead.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint for monthly cost trends so that HTTP clients can chart spend over time.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getCostTrends queries the PostgreSQL ci_snapshot data mart grouped by month for the last N months (costTrendsQuerySchema validates months 1-36, default 6) and returns 200 {success:true,data,count}. Unreachable because the tbmRoutes router is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); cannot be called.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint to allocate a CI's cost to targets so that non-GraphQL clients can run allocations.
expected_behavior: TBMController.allocateCosts validates sourceId/targetType/targetIds, reads the source CI cost from Neo4j (404 if missing), and computes equal/usage_based/direct allocations; it explicitly does NOT persist (TODO at controller line 352) and returns 200 with the computed allocations. Unreachable because tbm.routes.ts is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); also non-persisting stub, but primary defect is it is unreachable.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint to read a CI's allocations so that HTTP clients can audit chargeback.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getCostAllocations is a placeholder that returns 200 {success:true,data:{ciId,allocations:[],totalAllocatedCost:0}} (TODO at controller line 381, no DB query). On top of being a stub, it is unreachable because tbm.routes.ts is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); handler is also an empty placeholder.

user_story: As a TBM analyst, I want a REST endpoint to import GL data so that HTTP clients can upload financials.
expected_behavior: TBMController.importGLData is a placeholder returning 200 {success:true, message:'GL import feature coming soon'} (TODO at controller line 408, no parsing/persistence). It is also unreachable because tbm.routes.ts is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); handler is also an unimplemented stub.

user_story: As a software asset manager, I want a REST endpoint to list software licenses so that HTTP clients can review licensing.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getLicenses matches (:CI {type:'software'}) in Neo4j with optional vendor/status filters (licenseQuerySchema), ordered by expiry ASC LIMIT 100, returning 200 {success:true,data,count}. Unreachable because tbm.routes.ts is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); endpoint is dead.

user_story: As a software asset manager, I want a REST endpoint for upcoming renewals so that HTTP clients can plan renewals.
expected_behavior: TBMController.getUpcomingRenewals returns software CIs whose license_expiry_date falls between today and today + N days (renewalsQuerySchema validates days 1-365, default 90), ordered ascending, as 200 {success:true,data,count}. Unreachable because tbm.routes.ts is not mounted in server.ts.
issue_note: Route on unmounted tbmRoutes (tbm.routes.ts not imported/mounted in server.ts); endpoint cannot be reached.
